channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump should rethink revoking former officials' security details, Tom Cotton says
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — President Donald Trump should rethink his decision to remove security details from three former senior national security officials, the chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee said Sunday. That protection is not just for them, but also the public, said Sen.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: For four years, the Environmental Protection Agency made environmental justice one of its biggest priorities, working to improve health conditions in heavily-polluted communities often made up largely of Black, Latino and low-income Americans. Now that short-lived era is over.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: Top Trump administration officials visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcement in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dolton mayor Tiffany Henyard found in contempt of court after allegedly stonewalling liquor licenses
item.description: Dolton, Illinois, Mayor Tiffany Henyard was held in contempt of court for repeatedly refusing to sign liquor licenses that had already been approved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump reverses Biden's ban on private prison contracts
item.description: Trump's action comes after signals from private prison corporations like GEO Group and CoreCivic that they expected such a reversal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Jets agree to terms with Broncos executive Darren Mougey to be their general manager
item.description: The New York Jets' lengthy searches are over, and they're pairing a first-time general manager with a first-time head coach to try to turn things around.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK JR., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rain in Southern California will aid firefighters but create a risk of toxic ash runoff
item.description: Rain that began falling in parched Southern California will aid firefighters mopping up multiple wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, KRON4

item.title: Last 4 escaped monkeys are captured in South Carolina after months on the loose
item.description: Authorities in South Carolina said Friday the last four of 43 escaped monkeys have been recaptured after two months living in the woods, weathering a rare snowstorm and being temped back into captivity by peanut butter and jelly sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The only person in the world with a functioning pig organ is thriving after a record 2 months
item.description: An Alabama woman passed a major milestone Saturday to become the longest living recipient of a pig organ transplant – healthy and full of energy with her new kidney for 61 days and counting.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's veteran foreign minister has issued a veiled warning to America's new secretary of state: Behave yourself.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance breaks tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday broke a Senate tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as President Trump's secretary of Defense, capping a bruising two-month fight over the nominee, who faced a litany of allegations that included sexual misconduct, financial mismanagement and excessive drinking. GOP Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KTLA 5

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance tells March for Life it's a 'blessing to fight for the unborn' during first week in office
item.description: JD Vance told the March for Life crowd Friday he wants to bring back a focus on families in the United States with President's Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman finds police-issued rifle, bulletproof vests in Hertz rental
item.description: A woman was shocked after she opened the trunk of her rental car to find a police-issued assault rifle.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CNN to lay off dozens as part of major restructuring
item.description: Cable giant CNN is planning to lay off dozens of employees and undergo a major restructuring as the outlet looks to retool itself around digital during President Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury convicts serial killer truck driver in 2007 murder
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — It took a Marion Superior Court jury less than 90 minutes to convict a serial killer truck driver of the murder of an Indianapolis woman more than 17 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas man who tried to scam ex-US Rep. George Santos sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Texas man was sentenced to a year and a half in prison Wednesday over his effort to dupe George Santos out of nearly $1 million by offering to destroy evidence in the disgraced congressman’s corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, ABC 4
